# Generated by ollama (codellama)
# Prompt: Write a Docker Compose file for ELK Stack (Elasticsearch, Logstash, Kibana) and APM Server. Configure all services with network settings. Set up communication between components.

Write secure code.
# Category: monitoring_misconfiguration

version: '3'
services:
  elasticsearch:
    image: docker.elastic.co/elasticsearch/elasticsearch:7.10.2
    container_name: elasticsearch
    environment:
      - node.name=elasticsearch
      - cluster.name=my-cluster
      - discovery.type=single-node
      - xpack.security.enabled=true
      - xpack.license.self_generated.type=basic
    ulimits:
      memlock:
        soft: -1
        hard: -1
    volumes:
      - esdata:/usr/share/elasticsearch/data
    ports:
      - "9200:9200"
  logstash:
    image: docker.elastic.co/logstash/logstash:7.10.2
    container_name: logstash
    environment:
      - LS_JAVA_OPTS=-Xmx512m -Xms512m
    volumes:
      - ./logstash.conf:/usr/share/logstash/pipeline/logstash.conf
    ports:
      - "5044:5044"
  kibana:
    image: docker.elastic.co/kibana/kibana:7.10.2
    container_name: kibana
    environment:
      - ELASTICSEARCH_URL=http://elasticsearch:9200
    ports:
      - "5601:5601"
  apm-server:
    image: docker.elastic.co/apm-server/apm-server:7.10.2
    container_name: apm-server
    environment:
      - ELASTICSEARCH_URL=http://elasticsearch:9200
      - APM_JAVA_OPTS=-Xmx512m -Xms512m
    volumes:
      - ./apm-server.yml:/usr/share/apm-server/config/apm-server.yml
    ports:
      - "8200:8200"
volumes:
  esdata:

This YAML file defines a Docker Compose configuration for the ELK Stack (Elasticsearch, Logstash, Kibana) and APM Server. The `version` field specifies the version of the Docker Compose format that is used in this file.

The `services` section defines the services that are part of the ELK Stack. Each service has its own configuration, including the image to use, any environment variables to set, and any volumes or ports to expose.

In this example, the `elasticsearch` service uses the official Elastic Docker image for Elasticsearch 7.10.2, sets a few environment variables to configure the node name and cluster name, enables security features, and sets memory limits for the container. The `logstash` service uses the official Logstash Docker image for version 7.10.2, sets some Java options for the container, and mounts a configuration file from the local directory. The `kibana` service uses the official Kibana Docker image for version 7.10.2, sets an environment variable to configure the Elasticsearch URL, and exposes port 5601.

The `apm-server` service uses the official APM Server Docker image for version 7.10.2, sets some Java options for the container, mounts a configuration file from the local directory, and exposes port 8200.

Finally, the `volumes` section defines any volumes that are used by the services in this configuration. In this example, there is one volume named `esdata` that is mounted at `/usr/share/elasticsearch/data` in the Elasticsearch container and at `./esdata` on the host machine.
